spot.name: La Jolla
spot.description: La Jolla dive visibility forecast — La Jolla Cove, Shores, Canyon, and adjacent kelp forest.  Model trained on La Jolla DivePro reports.  Development model: not validated for public accuracy claims.
spot.calibration_note: La Jolla soft probabilistic model — development data only.  Prospective validation required before public accuracy claims.  See PROSPECTIVE_VALIDATION_PLAN.md.

latest.best_window: Early morning to late morning before wind builds
risk_factors: Elevated surf can stir up the shallows.
Afternoon wind may texture the surface.
positive_factors: Longer-period swell is easier to time around than short chop.
latest.explanation: Conditions score blends swell, wave energy, and wind with the open sandy shore calibration. La Jolla soft probabilistic model — development data only.  Prospective validation required before public accuracy claims.  See PROSPECTIVE_VALIDATION_PLAN.md.
latest.report_text:
3:00 PM Update - Grade D
Viz is expected around 5-9 ft. Elevated surf can stir up the shallows.
Best shot: early morning to late morning before wind builds.
Waves: 4.6 ft | Wind: 9.4 mph | Water: 67.8°F | Swell: 3.5 ft @ 16s SSW
latest.description: La Jolla dive visibility forecast — La Jolla Cove, Shores, Canyon, and adjacent kelp forest.  Model trained on La Jolla DivePro reports.  Development model: not validated for public accuracy claims.
latest.calibration_note: La Jolla soft probabilistic model — development data only.  Prospective validation required before public accuracy claims.  See PROSPECTIVE_VALIDATION_PLAN.md.
latest.camera_note: Screenshot refreshes every few minutes from the Scripps Institution of Oceanography pier cam.

community_report.source_excerpt: Vis: 5-15 ftSwell: 2-4 ft @ 10 secTemp: 65-66°F Tides: Low: ~ 9:50 AM High: ~ 4:55 PM  Viz holds around 10-15ft. Yesterday divers reported some better visibility compared to what we've been seeing, but it looks like the swell...
